question:
Solve the following arithmetic problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

Sally Draper gave her dad Don Draper 10oz of rum on his pancakes. Don can consume a maximum of 3 times that amount of rum for a healthy diet. If he already had 12oz of rum earlier that day, how many oz of rum can Don have after eating all of the rum and pancakes?
response:
Okay, so Don had 10oz of rum on his pancakes. He can have up to 3 times that amount for a healthy diet. I think 3 times 10 is 30, so the max he can have is 30oz.

But Don already had 12oz earlier. So, I subtract 12 from 30 to see how much more he can have. Umm, 30 - 12 is...18? So, I think he can have 18oz more after eating the pancakes. Did I do that right?
